pr.title: Fix aobj pointing to a hardcoded offset of r2
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Replaced a hardcoded small-data-area r2 offset in the inline assembly for HSD_AObjAlloc with a named extern float label, adding the missing extern declaration for lbl_804DE43C in src/sysdolphin/baselib/aobj.c.
what_changed: Added `extern const f32 lbl_804DE43C;` near the existing float externs in `aobj.c`.
Changed the `HSD_AObjAlloc` inline assembly load from `lfs f0, -0x15A4(r2)` to `lfs f0, lbl_804DE43C(r2)`.
The raw instruction bytes and address comment remained unchanged, indicating this was a symbolic/reference cleanup rather than a codegen behavior change.
smart_moves: Kept the original instruction encoding comment `C0 02 EA 5C` intact while replacing only the assembly operand with the symbol name.
Grouped the new float extern with nearby `lbl_804DE438`, preserving local organization of SDA float constants.
Avoided relying on a brittle numeric r2 offset when a named label could express the same address.
decomp_lessons: When inline assembly references the small data area through r2, prefer a known label such as `lbl_804DE43C(r2)` over a raw signed offset like `-0x15A4(r2)`.
Adding extern declarations for unresolved constants can make assembly snippets more relocatable and searchable without changing matched machine code.
For decompilation work, symbolic SDA operands document which constant is being loaded and reduce the risk of confusion when labels or linker layout evolve.
naming_conventions: Unidentified data symbols use address-derived labels such as `lbl_804DE43C`.
Sysdolphin baselib object functions follow names like `HSD_AObjAlloc`.
Extern constants in this area are declared with explicit types such as `extern const f32`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replace hardcoded r2-relative numeric operands with symbol-relative operands while preserving the same instruction bytes.
Use `lbl_804DE43C(r2)` style syntax for SDA references in inline PowerPC assembly.
A one-line operand substitution can improve source maintainability while maintaining an exact assembly match.
follow_up_queries: Find other inline assembly operands using raw negative r2 offsets that could be replaced with `lbl_*` SDA symbols.
Check nearby sysdolphin baselib files for float constants declared only by hardcoded offsets instead of extern labels.
Audit `aobj.c` for remaining unidentified labels that could be typed as `const f32` or moved into shared headers once known.
